content: When sibling builder branches have cross-file dependencies (e.g., CLI router imports commands created on another branch), the reviewer cannot run full quality gates in isolation. Merge order matters: the branch creating new modules must be merged before the branch importing them.

name: multi-feature-decomposition
description: Lead agent orchestration for multi-feature startup: decompose into 4 parallel builders with non-overlapping file scopes, then spawn a 5th integration builder for cross-cutting CLI routing changes. Send merge_ready per-builder as each reviewer passes rather than batching.

name: multi-builder-feature-orchestration
description: Lead orchestration pattern for multi-builder feature: 2 scouts (server/UI + types/agent), 3 builders with non-overlapping file scope (agent-def, types+routes+tests, view+app), 1 reviewer per builder, merge_ready sent per-builder as each passes review.

content: Leads send mail to 'orchestrator' not 'coordinator'. When checking for lead completion mail as coordinator, search with --from <lead-name> instead of --to coordinator to catch all messages.

Lead agents cannot write/edit files (hook-enforced). Single-file UI tasks that a lead would otherwise do directly must be delegated to a builder. Use legio spec write --body for spec creation instead of Write tool.

name: single-file-ui-lead
description: Single-file UI feature lead pattern: when task is 1 file with clear requirements and mulch expertise available, skip scout (all 3 exception conditions met), write detailed spec with acceptance criteria + style guide + technical context, spawn 1 builder, then spawn reviewer on worker_done. Total: 1 builder + 1 reviewer for a focused UI enhancement.

content: When removing a DI-injected parameter from a function signature (e.g., autopilot? from handleApiRequest), update: (1) the function definition, (2) the RoutesModule type annotation in dynamic import blocks, (3) all call sites in tests that passed the removed param followed by remaining params -- shift remaining params left.

name: backend-frontend-parallel-decomposition
description: Lead decomposition for UI feature spanning backend+frontend: split into 2 parallel builders (1: store+routes+types+tests, 2: view+nav). Frontend builder works from API contract spec, doesn't need backend to be merged first. Both pass review independently, then merge sequentially.

name: Lead decomposition for mechanical fixes
description: When spec is crystal-clear and changes are mechanical across a few files, skip scout and use single builder. When reviewer finds spec is incomplete, spawn targeted second builder for the gap rather than having first builder redo work.

content: Auto-nudge in legio mail send fires for ALL message types (including worker_done) — it writes a pending nudge marker and sends a direct tmux nudge if the recipient is idle. No manual legio nudge step is needed in builder/lead completion protocols.
Lead no-poll: leads must not sleep-loop for mail. After dispatching workers, output status and stop. Auto-nudge wakes lead via tmux when builders send worker_done.

title: Always delegate through leads, not direct builders
rationale: Leads handle review cycles, quality gates, and merge-ready signaling. Direct --force-hierarchy builder spawns skip review and put all coordination burden on coordinator.

content: Biome ignores the .legio/ directory (configured in biome.json). All worktrees live under .legio/worktrees/, so biome check returns 0 files processed (exit code 1) when run from a worktree. Browser JS views (src/server/public/views/) are also outside tsc coverage. For worktree agents: (a) run tsc/vitest from canonical repo root for TypeScript files, (b) verify browser JS manually against existing file conventions. Browser JS files have no automated quality gate in worktrees.

Pre-existing quality gate failures (typecheck errors, lint errors, test failures) in files outside the builder's FILE_SCOPE are not attributable to the builder and do not block a reviewer PASS verdict. This applies to sibling-owned files, files on other unmerged branches, and non-code-only tasks (README, docs).

name: skip-scout-exceptions
description: Scout phase can be skipped when all 3 conditions are met: (a) exact files are known, (b) scope is small (<=4 files), (c) patterns are well-documented in mulch. Valid for: mechanical fixes, doc-only changes, focused debugging, small UI features with documented patterns.

name: lead-lint-fix-escalation
description: Lead agent lint-fix pattern: when a builder completes with lint errors and exits before revision feedback arrives, the lead can run 'bunx biome check --write' on the builder's worktree to fix formatting/import issues, then ask the orchestrator to commit since lead hooks block git add/commit.

content: Lead agent revision cycle for lint-only failures: when reviewer returns FAIL on lint only (logic PASS) and builder has exited, note the auto-fixable lint issue in merge_ready message for coordinator/merger to apply post-merge rather than spawning a new builder.

Coordinator redirect/un-redirect handling: when coordinator sends contradictory instructions (redirect then un-redirect), cancel spawned agents immediately, create fresh beads, and re-spawn. Do not reuse cancelled bead IDs — create new ones for the revised work.

Chat consolidation: single builder for 4 tightly-coupled files (app.js, coordinator-chat.js, routes.ts, routes.test.ts) works well when frontend depends on backend endpoint — parallel builders would have cross-file deps. Confirms mulch mx-dde826: single builder suffices for tightly coupled extraction.
Reviewer worktrees cannot git checkout to builder branches (hooks block it), so reviewers do code inspection rather than running quality gates directly. Orchestrator must run gates before merge.

Lead revision cycle for import/prop errors: when reviewer catches wrong import path or wrong prop interface, respawn a fix builder with a focused spec listing the exact line changes needed. The fix builder commits on a fresh branch that supersedes the original for merge.

When a builder worktree forks from main before a parallel branch was merged, reviewer may FAIL for 'missing' content that was added on the parallel branch. Verify the builder's commit scope (git show --stat HEAD) before acting on such FAILs — they are false positives if the content was never in the builder's scope.

CHANGELOG [Unreleased] reviews: verify spec-listed commit SHAs exist in git log (git show --stat <sha>) rather than trusting description text alone. Cross-check Added/Changed/Fixed item counts against spec.

Lead agent direct-to-builder shortcut: for single-file tasks with exact spec (known file, exact code changes, no cross-cutting concerns), skip scout phase and spawn builder directly. Saves scout overhead (~2-3 min) while spec correctness is ensured by the detailed coordinator spec.

name: two-file-mechanical-lead
description: Lead decomposition for mechanical fixes with 2 files and exact changes: skip scout (all 3 conditions met per mx-1c9cf3), spawn single builder with both files in scope, reviewer validates, merge_ready per-builder. Total cycle ~5min for trivial spec.

content: When reviewing markdown-only agent definition changes, biome lint gate fails with 'No files processed' from inside .legio/worktrees/ (biome.json excludes that dir). Treat as expected, not a blocker.

name: markdown-agent-def-lead
description: Lead decomposition for mechanical markdown-only changes to agent definitions: skip scout (exact files known, purely text additions, patterns clear from reading files), single builder for all files, reviewer spawn on worker_done. 10 total locations across 3 files completed in ~13 minutes.

content: Post-sling nudge pattern: after every legio sling call, spawner must immediately run 'legio nudge <child-name> --force' to ensure child agent activates. Defense-in-depth for waitForTuiReady timing issue. Documented in agents/lead.md, supervisor.md, coordinator.md.

SQLite-backed coordination system with 5 databases: mail.db (messaging), sessions.db (agent lifecycle+runs), events.db (tool events+timelines), metrics.db (token/cost tracking), merge-queue.db (FIFO merge ordering). All use WAL mode + busy_timeout for concurrent access.

name: uncommitted-builder-recovery
description: Builder agents that exit before committing: when builders make all file edits but exit before git add/commit (likely hitting max turns or cost limits), coordinator can recover by manually committing from the worktree using git -C .legio/worktrees/<agent> add/commit, then merging the branch normally.
